title: Adversarial-Validation Weighting With Harder CV Gates
summary: The data spans multiple years and the test distribution likely differs in race, driver, and regime mix.
rationale: The data spans multiple years and the test distribution likely differs in race, driver, and regime mix. Dataset-shift literature recommends adversarial validation to identify train rows closest to test, while scikit-learn explicitly recommends time-aware and group-aware splits to avoid optimistic validation.
implementation_hint: Train a train-vs-test classifier on the full feature set and use predicted test-likeness as capped sample weights for the main model. Accept changes only if they hold under both rolling TimeSeriesSplit-style evaluation and LeaveOneGroupOut by Year or Year_Race, not just a single random/group CV.
expected_effect: May slightly reduce overfit to stale regimes and make local CV closer to eventual leaderboard behavior.
risk: If shift is weak, reweighting can hurt AUC by discarding useful older rows. Needs capped weights and ablation versus unweighted training.
